description: pydns is a Python DNS client library that provides both high and low level access to the DNS protocol. Older API than dnspython.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/pyDNS && python3 pyDNS.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/pyDNS && python3 pyDNS.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/pyDNS && python3 pyDNS.py --help`. DNS client (older API, still used).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the recon category. Fits when the engagement needs: DNS client (older API, still used).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: DNS client (older API, still used).

name: pyDNS

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
